results.name: Humana
results.key_finding: New HHS OIG report (Jun 12-15 2026) found Humana denied 72% of long-term care hospital and 54% of inpatient rehab admission requests under Medicare Advantage — far above the ~42% peer average — with ~95% of appealed denials overturned, indicating systematic inverse-prioritization of the highest-need post-acute patients; drives ACT 3.5->2.5, EQU 2.5->2.0, EMP/ACC/INT 3.0->2.5, SYS 3.5->3.0. Stays Functional (lower boundary).

results.name: Cargill
results.key_finding: May 20 2026 lockout of ~1,700 Teamsters at Fort Morgan CO (denied prayer breaks, bathroom-break retaliation, $0.70/hr initial offer) plus an ongoing DOJ beef-antitrust probe reinforce the already-Developing-floor labor/internal-consistency profile; pre-adjudication, near floor, no scorable downside.

results.name: Ford Motor
results.key_finding: The flagged ~9,000-worker UAW Kentucky Truck Plant strike was AVERTED — UAW Local 862 reached a tentative agreement addressing health/safety, ergonomics, and skilled trades. A resolved strike notice is a process event, not a scorable harm. No movement.

results.name: Philippines
results.key_finding: ICC confirmed 3 crimes-against-humanity counts against FORMER president Duterte (trial Nov 30 2026) for 2016-2022 drug-war killings; killings continue under Marcos at a reduced pace (1,173 since Jul 2022) with a near-total domestic accountability gap and ICC non-cooperation. Consistent with published 32.8; Duterte trial is external accountability, not state-driven repair.

results.name: Cuba
results.key_finding: April 2026 release of 2,010 prisoners included only 2 political prisoners and none of Amnesty's prisoners of conscience; 700+ political prisoners remain under surveillance/threat of re-arrest. Continuation, not escalation, of documented repression already behind the 32.8 (ACC 1.5, INT 1.5). Sub-threshold.

results.name: Uganda
results.key_finding: 19 confirmed Bundibugyo Ebola cases / 2 deaths linked to the DRC outbreak under an active PHEIC; WHO-supported surveillance, contact tracing, and cross-border response with US ($112M) and EU funding. A weak but functioning, internationally-backed state response — consistent with the Developing score.

results.name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
results.key_finding: Bundibugyo Ebola escalating (837 cases / 196 deaths as of Jun 15; Africa CDC warns potential worst-in-history; burial team attacked, 5 hostaged in Mongbwalu). Reinforces near-floor Critical; floor-limitation methodology -> no composite delta. NOTE: scanner carried 20.3; LIVE index authoritative at 2.3.

results.name: Iran
results.key_finding: 784 executions YTD 2026, 125th consecutive weekly prison protest across 56 facilities, Jun 16 protest-linked executions, Amnesty-documented torture. Reinforces near-floor Critical; no headroom for further scorable downgrade.

results.name: Russia
results.key_finding: Overnight Jun 17-18 strikes on Ukraine (212 drones, 4 ballistic missiles; Sumy 3 civilians killed; UN: highest civilian-fatality month in 3 years). Harm attributed to Russia. Already at absolute harm-flag floor (0.0).

results.name: Israel
results.key_finding: OCHA Jun 12 2026: 3,269 ceasefire violations, 77% of Gaza acutely food insecure, 150,000+ children needing acute malnutrition treatment, only 36% of allocated aid trucks entered. Harm attributed to Israel. Already at absolute harm-flag floor (0.0).

results.name: India
results.key_finding: HRW (Jun 16 2026) documents BSF unlawfully expelling ethnic Bengali Muslims to Bangladesh (200+ in 21 pushbacks since Jun 1; 1,500+ since May 7; ~100 Rohingya to Myanmar) under a 'detect, delete, deport' policy. Reinforces already-Critical score; sub-threshold for India's own composite.

results.name: Bolivia
results.key_finding: Week-6 protests (10 dead, 37 injured, 103 blockades, $1.6B losses); enhanced emergency powers passed Jun 15 but army authorized NOT deployed. Same enabling-framework basis that set 6.3 on Jun 9 (prior -6.5 proposal already APPLIED) and was confirmed Jun 15. Near floor, no downside room.

results.name: Burkina Faso
results.key_finding: Government/allied forces killed >2x as many civilians as Islamist militants since 2023 (likely war crimes/CAH per HRW/ACLED); Sahel-wide food insecurity. Reinforces near-floor Critical; no scorable headroom.

results.name: Mali
results.key_finding: Mali forces and Russian Africa Corps killed 3-4x more civilians than jihadists (summary executions, airstrikes, rape; 2026 offensives escalating). Reinforces Critical score; state complicity consistent with published 12.5. No threshold crossing.

results.name: Ukraine
results.key_finding: Overnight Jun 17-18 strikes and the Jun 15 Dormition Cathedral hit (5 killed) are harm inflicted ON Ukraine BY Russia — victim attribution to Russia, not Ukrainian state conduct. Ukraine's own profile unchanged; confirms at Functional 50.0.

results.name: Golden Entertainment
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; no compassion-relevant evidence in the 14-day window. Confirms at placeholder-grade Developing 25.0 (low-confidence desk review).

results.name: P.A.M. Transport
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; no fresh adverse evidence. Confirms at placeholder-grade Developing 25.0 (low-confidence desk review).

results.name: Guitar Center
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; no fresh adverse evidence (2020 bankruptcy is a stale artifact). Confirms at placeholder-grade Developing 25.0 (low-confidence desk review).

results.name: Neiman Marcus
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; no fresh adverse evidence (Saks Global ownership is a catalog-hygiene note). Confirms at placeholder-grade Developing 25.0 (low-confidence desk review).

results.name: Rite Aid
results.key_finding: Rotation backfill; no fresh adverse evidence (prior bankruptcy/store-closure history already in the score). Confirms at placeholder-grade Developing 25.0 (low-confidence desk review).

notes: 20 entities assessed (15 priority + 5 rotation backfill). One threshold-crossing change proposal: Humana (51.6 -> 40.6, -11.0, downgrade, medium confidence) driven by the new Tier-5 HHS OIG report (Jun 12-15 2026) documenting 72% LTCH / 54% IRF Medicare Advantage prior-authorization denial rates far above peers, with ~95% of appealed denials overturned — a systematic inverse-prioritization of the highest-need post-acute patients. Stays within the Functional band (lower boundary); no band change. All 19 other entities confirm at published composites (delta 0.0). Floor-limitation methodology applied to the at/near-floor critical entities (Russia 0.0, Israel 0.0 harm-flag floors; Iran 2.5, DRC 2.3, Bolivia 6.3, Burkina Faso 6.3 near-floor): fresh adverse evidence reinforces but cannot lower scores already at/near floor. Two scanner corrections handled: (1) DRC's scanner-carried 20.3 was overridden by the LIVE-index-authoritative 2.3; (2) Ford's flagged Kentucky Truck Plant strike was verified as AVERTED via tentative agreement, not a scorable harm. Philippines' Duterte ICC trial was treated as external accountability, not state-driven repair. Canonical-formula (computeCompositeFromDimensions, v1.2) reconstruction of every published baseline agreed within 0.5 points (Humana published recomputed to exactly 51.6); no math-hygiene issues. The 5 rotation-backfill F500 entries are placeholder-grade flat-2.0/flat-25.0 scores flagged informally for future evidence-based refinement.
